attributes.description: Alerts when the gateway itself returns HTTP 5xx without reaching a backend (backend status code is null), above 1% of total requests over a 15-minute window. Targets AGW-side incidents: WAF blocks resulting in 5xx, listener misconfiguration, TLS failures, or no-healthy-backend conditions.
attributes.name: [Azure Application Gateway OTel] AGW-originated 5xx rate

investigation_guide.blob:
## AGW-Originated 5xx Rate

The Application Gateway is responding 5xx to clients without reaching the backend. This is an AGW-side incident, not a backend application incident.

### Triage Steps
1. Open the Application Gateway Overview dashboard, scoped to the affected `cloud.resource_id`.
2. Inspect the `attributes.error.type` distribution for AGW reason codes — likely `ERRORINFO_NO_HEALTHY_BACKEND`, `ERRORINFO_UPSTREAM_*`, or TLS/protocol-related codes.
3. Check whether all listeners are affected (AGW-wide capacity / config issue) or only one (per-listener / per-rule problem).
4. Sample raw failures:
   `FROM logs-azure.application_gateway.otel-default | WHERE attributes.azure.category == "ApplicationGatewayAccessLog" AND attributes.http.response.status_code >= 500 AND attributes.azure.agw.backend.status_code IS NULL | KEEP @timestamp, attributes.error.type, attributes.azure.agw.listener.name, attributes.azure.agw.rule.name, attributes.url.path | SORT @timestamp DESC | LIMIT 50`
5. Review recent Azure platform alerts and Application Gateway capacity-unit metrics.

### Common Causes
- All backend pool members failing health probes (no healthy backend).
- WAF Prevention-mode blocks at scale.
- AGW capacity exhaustion (v2 autoscale upper bound reached).
- Listener/SSL configuration drift after a deploy.

### Tuning
- The 1% threshold is aggressive because AGW-side 5xx are rare in a healthy gateway. Raise it if your environment has elevated baseline AGW errors.
- Raise the 50-request minimum to avoid noise on low-traffic gateways.

esqlQuery.esql:
FROM logs-azure.application_gateway.otel-default
| WHERE attributes.azure.category == "ApplicationGatewayAccessLog"
// Client saw 5xx but AGW never reached a backend — gateway-side failure
| EVAL agw_internal_5xx = CASE(
    attributes.http.response.status_code >= 500
      AND attributes.azure.agw.backend.status_code IS NULL,
    1, 0)
| STATS
    total = COUNT(*),
    agw_errors = SUM(agw_internal_5xx)
  BY resource.attributes.cloud.resource_id
// Minimum sample size to avoid noise on idle gateways
| WHERE total > 50
| EVAL agw_error_rate_pct = ROUND(agw_errors / TO_DOUBLE(total) * 100.0, 2)
// Tight threshold: AGW-side 5xx should be near-zero in steady state
| WHERE agw_error_rate_pct > 1.0
| SORT agw_error_rate_pct DESC
| LIMIT 10
